title: regex cache lookup allocates key string per match
description: evaluator.rs str_matches_regex() and str_matches_regex_pat(): every call allocates one or two temporary Strings to form the cache lookup key (e.g. format!("(?s)\\A(?:{pattern})\\z") then format!("(?i){anchored}")). The cache was designed to amortize regex compilation, but the key construction still allocates on every evaluated match. The fix is to change the cache key scheme: store patterns under their raw form (e.g. (pattern, comparator) as a struct key or a pre-canonical string), or pass the pre-formed cache key in from the caller who already has it (str_matches_glob already passes base_key). Alternatively use a Cow<str> or &str key with a HashMap<String, ...> using entry API tricks. This affects hot eval path for scripts using :regex or :matches.
